title: Geracium aureum
intro: Geranium aureum, also known as the Golden Geranium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae, characterized by its vibrant yellow flowers and attractive foliage. The health benefits of Geranium aureum are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin problems, wounds, and menstrual disorders. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Geranium aureum include tinctures, infusions, and essential oils, which are often used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
taxonomy: Geranium aureum is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Geraniaceae. This family is commonly known as the geranium or storksbill family. The genus Geranium is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Northern Hemisphere, with some species found in tropical regions. The Geraniaceae family is a part of the order Geraniales, which also includes other flowering plant families. The classification of Geranium aureum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
uses: Geranium aureum, also known as the golden geranium, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes.
